domainRules.property: MINIMAL - specificity, clarity, factual urgency only. No persuasion frameworks.
domainRules.personal: FULL ACCESS - storytelling, identity, emotional resonance, reflection.
domainRules.professional: SELECTIVE - authority, education, proof, clarity. No personal narratives.

self-improvement.instruction: Emphasize growth and becoming better. Connect to the reader's desire to improve.

status.instruction: Highlight achievement, recognition, and progress. Make the reader feel proud.

security.instruction: Focus on stability, safety, and smart decisions. Reduce perceived risk.

belonging.instruction: Create connection and community. Make the reader feel part of something.

curiosity.instruction: Spark interest and desire to learn more. Create intrigue without clickbait.

fear-of-missing-out.instruction: Subtly highlight opportunity cost without being pushy or manipulative.

trust.instruction: Build credibility through expertise and proof. Be reliable and consistent.

aspiration.instruction: Paint an inspiring future. Connect to dreams and possibilities.

story-lesson-takeaway.instruction: Open with a specific moment → Share the experience → Extract the lesson → End with a clear takeaway.
story-lesson-takeaway.template: HOOK (specific moment) → STORY (what happened) → LESSON (what you learned) → TAKEAWAY (what they can apply)

problem-agitation-solution.instruction: Name the problem → Amplify the pain point → Present the solution.
problem-agitation-solution.template: PROBLEM (identify the pain) → AGITATION (why it matters/costs) → SOLUTION (your answer)

hook-value-cta.instruction: Strong opening hook → Deliver immediate value → Clear call-to-action.

insight-proof-application.instruction: Share the insight → Back it with evidence → Show how to apply it.
insight-proof-application.template: INSIGHT (the key point) → PROOF (data/evidence) → APPLICATION (how to use it)

challenge-solution-result.instruction: Describe the challenge → Explain the approach → Reveal the outcome.
challenge-solution-result.template: CHALLENGE (what they faced) → SOLUTION (how you helped) → RESULT (the outcome)

curiosity-reveal-engage.instruction: Create curiosity → Reveal the interesting part → Invite engagement.
curiosity-reveal-engage.template: CURIOSITY (open loop) → REVEAL (the payoff) → ENGAGE (invite response)

specificity.description: Numbers, dates, concrete details instead of vague claims
specificity.instruction: Use specific numbers, dates, and concrete details. '3 days' beats 'a few days'. '$47,000' beats 'thousands'.

future-pacing.description: Paint the future state after taking action
future-pacing.instruction: Help the reader visualize their future state. Use present tense for the future.
examples: You're closing on your dream home...

power-words.description: Emotionally charged words that trigger response
power-words.instruction: Use emotionally charged words naturally: discover, transform, unlock, proven, essential, secret.

social-proof.instruction: Reference others' experiences or validation when relevant. Show you're not alone.

authority-markers.instruction: Subtly signal expertise through specific knowledge and insights, not claims.

bucket-brigades.description: Transition phrases that pull readers forward
bucket-brigades.instruction: Use transition phrases that keep readers moving: 'Here's the thing...', 'But wait...', 'The truth is...'
examples: But here's what most people miss:
And that's when it hit me:

open-loops.description: Create curiosity gaps that demand resolution
open-loops.instruction: Create curiosity gaps that make readers want to continue. Open a loop, then close it.
examples: I almost made a huge mistake.
There's one thing nobody tells you.

pattern-interrupt.instruction: Break expectations to capture attention. Say something unexpected.
examples: I'm going to tell you something most agents won't.

sensory-language.instruction: Use vivid, descriptive language that creates mental images. Appeal to senses.

contrast.instruction: Show before/after or compare options to highlight value. Make the difference tangible.
examples: Before: stressed about listings. After: 3 offers in 48 hours.
Most agents say X. Here's what actually works:

rhetorical-questions.description: Questions that engage and prompt reflection
rhetorical-questions.instruction: Ask questions that engage the reader and prompt reflection. Don't overuse.
examples: Ever feel like you're always one step behind?

staccato.description: Short, punchy sentences that hit hard
staccato.instruction: Use short sentences. Fragment when it works. Punch. Breathe. No run-ons. No fluff. Every word earns its place.
examples: No emails. No showings. Just rest.
The deal fell through. Then this happened.

reflective-engagement.instruction: End with a question that invites genuine reflection and response.
examples: What's been on your mind lately?

soft-invitation.instruction: Offer a gentle next step without pressure. Make it easy to say yes.
examples: Happy to chat if this resonates.

authority-positioning.description: Position as expert ready to help
authority-positioning.instruction: Position as an expert ready to help when they're ready. Confident but not pushy.
examples: Ready to work with someone who gets it?

community-building.instruction: Encourage interaction that builds community. Make them feel part of something.
examples: Tag someone who needs to hear this.

value-exchange.instruction: Offer additional value in exchange for engagement.
examples: Comment 'GUIDE' and I'll send you my checklist.

direct-action.description: Clear, direct call to take next step
direct-action.instruction: Clear, direct call to take the next step. No ambiguity.

numberHeadlines.description: Headlines with specific numbers perform 36% better
examples: 3 Things Every First-Time Buyer Should Know
How to Get Top Dollar for Your Home (Without Overpricing)
How I Helped 3 Families Find Their Forever Home This Month
Is Your Home Ready for 2024's Market?
What Would You Do With an Extra $50K?
The Market Just Shifted. Here's What It Means.
I Was Wrong About Open Houses.
This Changed How I Think About Pricing.

storyOpening.description: Start in the middle of action
storyOpening.instruction: Drop the reader into a moment. No preamble.
storyOpening.example: It was 11pm when my phone buzzed. 'We got it.'

contrarianOpening.instruction: Say something unexpected that makes them stop scrolling.
contrarianOpening.example: Most agents will tell you to price high. Here's why that's wrong.

questionOpening.instruction: Ask something that makes them think.
questionOpening.example: What would change if you knew exactly what buyers wanted?

dataOpening.instruction: Open with a surprising or relevant number.
dataOpening.example: 87% of buyers start their search online. Here's what they're looking for.

confessionOpening.instruction: Share something honest that humanizes you.
confessionOpening.example: I almost quit real estate in my first year. Here's what kept me going.
